title: 悬崖上的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\241-悬崖上的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After Frankie leaves the sanatorium, a nurse arrives to bring Bobby tea and remarks on Frankie's approachable nature, a sentiment Bobby silently disagrees with. While reading the *Marblehead Gazette*, Bobby becomes agitated and uses his call bell to demand that the nurse summon Frankie back immediately.

Upon her return, Bobby shows Frankie a blurred photograph in the newspaper of Mrs. Amelia Kemm, the sister of the deceased Alexander Prichard. Bobby realizes that this image is not the same photograph he previously found and placed back into the deceased's pocket. This discovery leads them to suspect that a man named Baxton-French (also referred to as Baskerville-French or Barnett-French) switched the photos. Bobby describes the man as a pleasant-voiced gentleman with no distinguishing features who had claimed to be looking for a place to stay. Frankie suggests they verify this man's identity through local real estate agents, Wheeler and Owen.

Frankie posits that if the death was a murder, Bobby’s discovery of the original photo made him a target. However, Bobby finds flaws in this theory, noting that he did not speak up immediately and that the timing of the photo's appearance in the newspaper was highly coincidental. Furthermore, Bobby insists that when he returned the photo to the pocket, Baxton-French was not present; the man arrived several minutes later, and Bobby could have heard him approaching from the only vantage point not obscured by the cliff. They conclude that there must be a different motive or a larger conspiracy involving the Kemm family.
